channel.title: guides on Christopher Hannan
channel.description: Recent content in guides on Christopher Hannan

item.title: Ansible and Cisco DevNet: Part 2. Playbooks and Git
Ansible and Cisco DevNet: Part 1. Getting Started
item.description: Table of Contents Objectives Prerequisites Guide Next Steps Objectives Install and verify the default Ansible settings Create a new Ansible environment Prerequisites Note: This guide assumes that you already have a control node that can run Ansible, with Python installed and access to the internet. Guide Step 1: Install Ansible Listed below are the installation steps for both Ubuntu and macOS. For other distributions, please follow Ansible’s Installation Guide.

item.title: Cisco ACI - Leaf Switch Replacement
item.description: Note: Before following any of the steps below, please take note of the POD ID, Node ID and Node Name of the switch to be replaced. When you unbox the new switch, also note down the serial number of the new switch. Power on the new switch and connect a console cable to check if it is running in ACI mode or NX-OS mode. If it is running in NX-OS mode, follow the steps documented in Cisco ACI - Converting NX-OS to ACI Boot Mode to convert the switch to ACI mode.

item.title: Cisco ACI - Converting NX-OS to ACI Boot Mode
item.description: Follow the steps below to convert a Cisco Nexus 9000 Series switch from NX-OS standalone mode to ACI boot mode. 1. Verify which mode the switch is running (ACI or NX-OS) Power on the new switch and connect a console to check if it is running in ACI mode or NX-OS mode. Cisco NX-OS filenames begin with “n9000” while ACI filenames begin with “aci-n9000”: switch# show version 2. Uploading the ACI image The ACI image can be copied to the switch directly via USB or via SCP from the APIC or an SCP server.
